introduction: Permutation counts ordered arrangements; combination counts unordered selections. Factorials, nPr = n!/(n−r)! and nCr = n!/[r!(n−r)!] underpin most counting problems in aptitude exams.
explanation:
CONCEPT EXPLANATION

Factorial: n! = n × (n−1) × … × 1; 0! = 1.

Permutation ⁿPᵣ = n!/(n−r)! — order matters.

Combination ⁿCᵣ = n!/[r!(n−r)!] — order does not matter.

Relationship: ⁿPᵣ = ⁿCᵣ × r!

Circular permutation of n distinct objects = (n−1)!

Arrangements with repetition: n^r (r slots, n choices each).

Arrangements of n objects with duplicates n!/(p!q!…).

Selection with conditions: use complementary counting or split into cases.

REAL-LIFE APPLICATIONS

Password strength, committee formation, seating plans, lottery draws, and DNA sequence counting in bioinformatics basics.

COMMON MISTAKES

1) Using permutation when order does not matter.
2) Forgetting to divide by factorials for repeated letters.
3) Treating circular like linear (not using (n−1)!).
4) Double-counting in complementary problems.
5) Confusing ⁿCᵣ with ⁿCₙ₋ᵣ (they are equal — use the smaller r for easier calculation).

IMPORTANT NOTES

• ⁿC₀ = ⁿCₙ = 1.
• ⁿCᵣ = ⁿCₙ₋ᵣ.
• At least one problems: total − none.
• Words with all letters: n! divided by factorial of each repeat count.
• Committee with mandatory member: fix that member, choose rest.

shortcutTips.title: Order test
shortcutTips.tip: If swapping two picks changes the outcome, use permutation.

shortcutTips.title: Smaller r
shortcutTips.tip: Compute ⁿCᵣ using the smaller of r and n−r.

shortcutTips.title: Circular fix
shortcutTips.tip: Fix one position in a circle to avoid overcounting rotations.

shortcutTips.title: At least one
shortcutTips.tip: Total arrangements minus arrangements with zero of that type.

shortcutTips.title: Repeated letters
shortcutTips.tip: Divide by factorial of each repeated letter count.

shortcutTips.title: nCr on calculator
shortcutTips.tip: ⁿCᵣ = ⁿPᵣ / r! — divide permutation by r!.

solvedExamples.problem: In how many ways can 5 people sit in a row?
